Let’s say you want to read a book.

text_blocks.text: When data is requested, the goal is to read a word into a CPU register.

runs.text: When data is requested, the goal is to read a word into a CPU register.

text_blocks.text: The CPU first contacts the cache and asks if it has a copy.

runs.text: The CPU first contacts the cache and asks if it has a copy.

text_blocks.text: If it does… that is a cache hit, and, well, that was easy. Just copy that value into the register.

runs.text: If it does… that is a cache hit, and, well, that was easy. Just copy that value into the register.

text_blocks.text: If it does not, this is a cache miss.

runs.text: If it does not, this is a cache miss.

text_blocks.text: It will then contact the next component in the memory hierarchy. (RAM)

runs.text: It will then contact the next component in the memory hierarchy. (RAM)

text_blocks.text: When the CPU requests memory in an empty cache, the data obviously won’t be available locally.

runs.text: When the CPU requests memory in an empty cache, the data obviously won’t be available locally.

text_blocks.text: This is a compulsory miss, a “miss” due to the first access of a block of data.

runs.text: , a “miss” due to the first access of a block of data.

text_blocks.text: These are, as they suggest, completely unavoidable.

runs.text: These are, as they suggest, completely unavoidable.

text_blocks.text: When the CPU requests memory that happens to already be in the cache, the data is read locally (quickly).

runs.text: When the CPU requests memory that happens to already be in the cache, the data is read locally (quickly).

text_blocks.text: These avoid having to communicate at all with memory.

text_blocks.text: As the CPU requests memory, the cache will fill to satisfy each compulsory miss.

runs.text: As the CPU requests memory, the cache will fill to satisfy each compulsory miss.

text_blocks.text: When it fills up completely, it will have no further room for the next miss.

runs.text: When it fills up completely, it will have no further room for the next miss.

text_blocks.text: On a miss, it requests the data from memory.

runs.text: On a miss, it requests the data from memory.

text_blocks.text: Yet, where does it go?? We must remove one.

runs.text: Yet, where does it go?? We must remove one.

text_blocks.text: This is a capacity miss. The memory requirements of the program are larger than the cache.

runs.text: . The memory requirements of the program are larger than the cache.

text_blocks.text: It is difficult to know what block of data to omit from this cache on such a miss.

runs.text: It is difficult to know what block of data to omit from this cache on such a miss.

text_blocks.text: However we can exploit the common locality patterns of programs to improve our cache.

runs.text: However we can exploit the common locality patterns of programs to improve our cache.

text_blocks.text: There is temporal locality: accessed data is likely to be used again in near future.

runs.text: : accessed data is likely to be used again in near future.

text_blocks.text: This is what caches generally capture.

runs.text: This is what caches generally capture.

text_blocks.text: However, spatial locality is also likely: data is often grouped together.

runs.text: is also likely: data is often grouped together.

text_blocks.text: When we access a struct field, we will often access another which is nearby in memory.

runs.text: When we access a struct field, we will often access another which is nearby in memory.

text_blocks.text: We would like to keep data that is adjacent in memory in the cache, together, at the same time.

runs.text: We would like to keep data that is adjacent in memory in the cache, together, at the same time.

text_blocks.text: To do this, we “hash” the address. This is used to determine the cache slot.

runs.text: To do this, we “hash” the address. This is used to determine the cache slot.

text_blocks.text: Just a fancy way to say: we divide the address by the cache size and use the remainder.

text_blocks.text: Often using fast/small memories to act as a “cache” for slower ones.

runs.text: Often using fast/small memories to act as a “cache” for slower ones.

text_blocks.text: Caches can be arranged in several ways:

runs.text: Caches can be arranged in several ways:

text_blocks.text: Blocks go in particular slots (direct-mapped / 1-way associative)

runs.text: Blocks go in particular slots (direct-mapped / 1-way associative)

text_blocks.text: Hybrid: Blocks go to particular slots… but then can go in any bin in that slot.

runs.text: Hybrid: Blocks go to particular slots… but then can go in any bin in that slot.

text_blocks.text: Caches attempt to exploit temporal and spatial locality of programs.

runs.text: Caches attempt to exploit temporal and spatial locality of programs.

text_blocks.text: And even a slight improvement to hit rate can dramatically improve overall performance of a program!

runs.text: And even a slight improvement to hit rate can dramatically improve overall performance of a program!
